revision.text:
[[ja:藤白鈴木氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Fujishiro Suzuki clan (藤白鈴木氏) | crest = Dakiine.png | crestcaption = Dakiine (抱き稲) | surname = {{ill|Hozumi no Asomi|ja|穂積朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | founder = {{ill|Suzuki Motoyuki|ja|鈴木基行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Motoyuki|1=Suzuki Motoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20040850}} | category = {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}
{{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|qid=Q2927809}} | homeland = {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} (藤白浦, Nagusa District) | base = Kii Province, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} | notablemembers = {{ill|Suzuki Shigeie|ja|鈴木重家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigeie|1=Suzuki Shigeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219424}}, {{ill|Kamei Shigekiyo|ja|亀井重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamei Shigekiyo|1=Kamei Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370787}} | branches = {{ill|Mikawa Suzuki clan|ja|三河鈴木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikawa Suzuki clan|1=Mikawa Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17987398}} (samurai, gentry)
{{ill|Enashi Suzuki clan|ja|江梨鈴木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enashi Suzuki clan|1=Enashi Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17222885}} (samurai)
{{ill|Saika party Suzuki clan|ja|雑賀党鈴木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saika party Suzuki clan|1=Saika party Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17991597}} (samurai, gentry)
{{ill|Kamei clan|ja|亀井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamei clan|1=Kamei clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370753}} (samurai, nobility - {{ill|Count|en|Count|qid=Q3519259|lt=Count|1=Count}})
{{ill|Torii clan|en|Torii clan|qid=Q2975387|lt=Tori clan|1=Torii clan}} (samurai, nobility - {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|qid=Q185902|lt=Viscount|1=Viscount}})Though officially recognized as descendants of the {{ill|Kanmu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1079102}}, receiving the name from [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}. {{Cite book|date=1923|title=寛政重脩諸家譜 第3輯|url={{NDLDC|1082714/1}}|publisher=国民図書}}
{{ill|Doi clan|ja|土居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (samurai)
{{ill|Yamamoto clan|ja|山本氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto clan|1=Yamamoto clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985330}} (samurai) }} The '''Fujishiro Suzuki clan''' (藤白鈴木氏) was a Japanese {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} and {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|qid=Q2927809}} family. Their {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|qid=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}} was {{ill|Hozumi|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi|1=Hozumi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597079}}, and they were the main {{ill|Sōke|en|Sōke|qid=Q769323|lt=Sōke|1=Sōke}} of the Suzuki families that descended from the Hozumi clan. They served as priests at {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090605}}, a former {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{ill|Ōji shrine|ja|王子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kii Province. The family’s {{ill|Tsuji|ja|通字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q64026473}} was "Shige" (重). == Overview == The Fujishiro Suzuki clan descended from the Kumano branch of the Hozumi clan, tracing their lineage to {{ill|Hozumi Nomi no Mikoto|ja|穂積濃美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Nomi no Mikoto|1=Hozumi Nonimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129380}}, the son of {{ill|Hozumi Omi|ja|穂積老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Omi|1=Hozumi no Oyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129332}}. Their ancestor, {{ill|Suzuki Motoyuki|ja|鈴木基行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Motoyuki|1=Suzuki Motoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20040850}}, adopted the surname Suzuki, marking the clan's beginning.[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』古代氏族研究会、1986th year In the 12th century, the family relocated from Kumano to Fujishiro and became hereditary priests of the {{ill|Ōji shrine|ja|王子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now Fujishiro Shrine). As one of the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=}}, they held significant influence in the Kumano region, with successive heads titled "Suzuki Shōji." During the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, members such as {{ill|Suzuki Shigeie|ja|鈴木重家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigeie|1=Suzuki Shigeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219424}} and his brother {{ill|Kamei Shigekiyo|ja|亀井重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamei Shigekiyo|1=Kamei Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370787}} rose to prominence. They su11orted {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qid=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} during the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q737542}} and played roles in battles such as {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|qid=Q968676|lt=Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}}. They later joined Yoshitsune during his final stand at {{ill|Koromogawa|ja|衣川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koromogawa|1=Koromogawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626366}}, where they perished. After Shigeie’s death, his son {{ill|Suzuki Shigetsugu|ja|鈴木重次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigetsugu|1=Suzuki Shigetsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11649344}} inherited the leadership. Shigetsugu fought for the imperial court during the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|qid=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} and died in 1258. During the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|qid=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, {{ill|Suzuki Shigehiro|ja|鈴木重恒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigehiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} su11orted the {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1206988}} under Emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}. In 1392, {{ill|Suzuki Shigeyoshi|ja|鈴木重義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigeyoshi|1=Suzuki Shigeyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16769352}} died in battle under {{ill|Yamana Yoshinori|ja|山名義理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana Yoshinori|1=Yamana Yoshitada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11467618}} against {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshihiro|en|Ōuchi Yoshihiro|qid=Q4389583|lt=Ōuchi Yoshihiro|1=Ōuchi Yoshihiro}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, the family sided with {{ill|Kōsa|en|Kōsa|qid=Q1045296|lt=Honganji Kennyo|1=Kōsa}} during the {{ill|Ishiyama Hongan-ji War|en|Ishiyama Hongan-ji War|qid=Q859815|lt=Ishiyama Hongan-ji War|1=Ishiyama Hongan-ji War}}, losing their domains. In the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|qid=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}}, {{ill|Suzuki Shigetaka|ja|鈴木重興|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigetaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} fought for the Tokugawa and received exemptions from the {{ill|Asano clan|en|Asano clan|qid=Q2659895|lt=Asano clan|1=Asano clan}}. In 1942, the last head of the family, {{ill|Shigeyoshi Suzuki (film director)|en|Shigeyoshi Suzuki (film director)|qid=Q7496417|lt=Suzuki Jūkichi|1=Shigeyoshi Suzuki (film director)}}, died, and the Suzuki line of Fujishiro Shrine became extinct. == Restoration of the Suzuki Homestead == The Suzuki Homestead on the grounds of Fujishiro Shrine fell into disrepair and became vacant.{{Cite web|date=2022-05-15|title=和歌山：＜海南で上棟式＞鈴木屋敷　完成へ前進|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/local/wakayama/news/20220514-OYTNT50192/|website=読売新聞オンライン|publisher=[[Yomiuri Shimbun]]|accessdate=2022-06-26}} In 2015, it was designated a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}, prompting a restoration project funded by grants and crowdfunding. Restoration began in 2021 and was completed in March 2023.{{Cite web|date=2023-03-30|title=鈴木姓のルーツとされる「鈴木屋敷」、和歌山に復元|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230330-OYT1T50113/|website=読売新聞オンライン|publisher=読売新聞社|accessdate=2023-03-30}} The homestead was opened to the public on April 1, 2023.{{Cite web|date=2023-04-03|title=鈴木屋敷を復元 姓の発祥地で式典|url=https://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2023/04/20230402_115809.html|publisher=[[Wakayama Shimbun]]|accessdate=2023-04-03}} == Succession of Clan Heads == Direct lineage: * {{ill|Hozumi Nomi no Mikoto|ja|穂積濃美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Nomi no Mikoto|1=Hozumi Nonimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129380}} → {{ill|Hozumi Omi|ja|忍麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} → Ototsugu → Saemaro → Nagamori → Toyoniwa → Kunioki 1. {{ill|Suzuki Motoyuki|ja|鈴木基行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Motoyuki|1=Suzuki Motoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20040850}} (son of Kunioki) 2. {{ill|Suzuki Yoshifuji|ja|鈴木良氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Yoshifuji|1=Suzuki Yoshiuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20040851}} 3. {{ill|Suzuki Shigefuji|ja|鈴木重氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigefuji|1=Suzuki Shigeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20040841}} ... (further lineage continues) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[Sakamoto Tarō]], [[Hirano Kunio]], eds. 『日本古代氏族人名辞典』[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990. ISBN 4642022430 * [[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]] 『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』古代氏族研究会, 1986. * [[Suzuki Shinya|鈴木眞哉]] 『紀州雑賀衆鈴木一族』, 1984. * Kainan City Board of Education 『海南郷土史』, 1954. * Itō Ashiten 『稲毛郷土史』, 1970. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Suzuki clan|ja|鈴木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki clan|1=Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132396620}} * {{ill|Suzuki Homestead|ja|鈴木屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Homestead|1=Suzuki Yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219449}} * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Suzuki Shigeie|ja|鈴木重家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shigeie|1=Suzuki Shigeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219424}} == External Links == * {{Wayback |url=http://www.iris.dti.ne.jp/~min30-3/magoichi/fujisiro.htm |title=Fujishiro Suzuki Association Official Website |date=2013-12-03}} {{Fujishiro Suzuki clan heads}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[File:241006 Fujishiro-jinja Kainan Wakayama pref Japan30s3.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Shrine|ja|藤白神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Shrine|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090605}}, where the Fujishiro Suzuki clan served as priests]] [[File:241006 The Suzuki-homestead Kainan Wakayama pref Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|The {{ill|Suzuki Homestead|ja|鈴木屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Homestead|1=Suzuki Yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219449}} on the grounds of Fujishiro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|藤白鈴木氏|version=102198630|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:藤白鈴木氏]] {{wikidata link|Q18224450}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:History of Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Suzuki (surname)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
